---
title: "SDTM: Findings (Labs & Vital Signs)"
---

<!--
========================================================================
SKELETON + VERIFY LIST — prose NOT yet written. DO NOT COMMIT as final.

This is the Findings half of the split (Events -> sessions/sdtm-events.qmd,
already drafted). The F-list below was approved with the combined
events-findings VERIFY list; it is reproduced here as the working list
for this session's own drafting turn.

All [IG] claims PRE-VERIFIED against local SDTMIG v3.4; [CT] confirmed
live via sdtm.terminology at prose time.

This session INTRODUCES R/build_sdtm.R and data/sdtm/ (the persisted,
committed SDTM datasets), and ADDS lb + vs to build_sdtm.R (currently
it builds dm + ae only).

Registry defects for this session (distinct subjects):
  D5 out-of-range HbA1c, REAL  -> 101-052 (13.9% at SCREENING)
  D6 byte-perfect duplicate lb -> 101-010 (SCREENING HbA1c 8.2)
  D7 missing unit              -> 101-037 (GLUC WEEK 8; met in sdtm-concepts)

PROPOSED CENTRAL INTUITION (for approval, split from the combined one):
  "A Findings row records what a measurement was — its test and its
   result, kept twice as collected and standardised — and the domain's
   natural key is what makes a duplicate detectable and a real outlier
   keepable."

OPEN ITEM TO RAISE AT DRAFTING: GLPX raw lb_raw.csv carries NO reference
ranges (cols: subjid, sex, visit, colldt, test, result, unit). LBNRIND /
LBORNRLO / LBORNRHI (F4, needed for D5) require a reference range per
test. Decide the source: (a) add ref_lo/ref_hi to simulate_trial.R's lab
vendor file (realistic — vendors supply ranges), or (b) a documented
reference-range lookup in build_sdtm.R. Recommend (a). Author to decide
before F4/D5 can be built with real values.

========================================================================
VERIFY LIST (Findings) — every SDTM specific this session will assert.

F1 [IG §6.3.5.6 spec header] LB is Findings-class; ONE RECORD PER LAB
   TEST PER TIME POINT PER VISIT PER SUBJECT. -> D6 duplicate violates
   this natural key.

F2 [IG §6.3.5.6 spec; §4.5.1.1] Result quartet, all Expected: LBORRES,
   LBORRESU (UNIT), LBSTRESC, LBSTRESN, LBSTRESU (UNIT). Original
   preserved beside standardised.

F3 [IG §6.3.5.6 assumption 7] LBORRESU/LBSTRESU take the UNIT codelist;
   submit the CDISC Submission Value; unmapped -> synonym or Define-XML
   extension + new-term request. -> D7: 101-037 blank GLUC unit resolved
   to mmol/L from the vendor's documented panel, logged as a query (no
   fabricated confirmation — as approved).

F4 [IG §6.3.5.6 spec; assumption 3; §4.5.5] Reference-range vars, all
   Expected: LBORNRLO/HI, LBSTNRLO/HI, LBNRIND (codelist NRIND). LBNRIND
   is NOT clinical significance. -> D5: 101-052 SCREENING HbA1c 13.9%
   flagged HIGH and KEPT (real value, poorly controlled patient).
   BLOCKED on the reference-range source decision above.

F5 [IG §6.3.5.6 assumption 6; §4.1.8.1] Central-lab value is COLLECTED,
   not derived; LBDRVFL null. (Why D6 is a transmission artefact.)

F6 [IG §6.3.5.6 spec] Timing: LBDTC (Exp), LBDY (Perm), VISITNUM (Exp),
   VISIT (Perm). Study day via the DM RFSTDTC rule (§4.4.4).

F7 [IG §4.5.9; §6.3.5.6] Baseline: LBBLFL (Perm, backward-compat) vs
   LBLOBXFL (Exp) = "last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC".
   Authoritative analysis baseline is ADaM (forward-link to adsl).

F8 [IG §6.3.5.6 spec] LBSEQ: Required, unique within subject/domain.

V1 [IG §6.3.13 spec header] VS is Findings-class, ONE RECORD PER VITAL
   SIGN MEASUREMENT PER TIME POINT PER VISIT PER SUBJECT — structurally
   like LB. VSORRESU uses codelist VSRESU (NOT the generic UNIT). Mainly
   in the exercise; LB carries the worked example.

X2 [IG §6.3.5.6 header + natural key] De-duplication restores the
   one-record-per-key structure (D6).
X3 [IG §4.4.4] Study day reuses the DM study_day() rule.

[CT] to confirm live: NRIND (id + NORMAL/ABNORMAL/HIGH/LOW); VSRESU
(id + members); UNIT/mmol/L (confirmed); LBTESTCD (confirmed).

Build/infra for this session:
  - Add build_lb() and build_vs() to R/build_sdtm.R; regenerate and
    commit data/sdtm/{lb,vs}.csv.
  - Introduce data/sdtm/ and the traceability chain (raw -> build -> sdtm)
    pedagogically here, since after this session the SDTM set is complete.
========================================================================
-->

```{r setup}
#| include: false
#| eval: true
library(tidyverse)
library(sdtm.terminology)
```

::: {.callout-note}
### Session at a Glance

TODO: time budget once written. Sibling of
[Events](sdtm-events.qmd); resolves defects D5, D6 and D7 and
introduces the persisted SDTM datasets in `data/sdtm/`.
:::

## When Do You Use This? {#sec-sdtm-findings}

TODO (prose pass, after the reference-range decision): scenarios — a lab
transfer with a byte-perfect duplicate and a real out-of-range value; a
blank unit; and the moment you need a finished SDTM you can hand to the
analysis team. Builds on [SDTM Concepts](sdtm-concepts.qmd),
[The DM Domain](sdtm-dm.qmd) and [Events](sdtm-events.qmd).

## Learning Objectives

TODO after approval.

## Background: TODO — What a Findings Row Is

TODO after the reference-range source is decided.

## Example 1: TODO — Building LB (the result quartet, de-dup, units)

TODO.

## Example 2: TODO — Reference ranges, the real outlier, and baseline

TODO — blocked on reference-range source.

## What Can Go Wrong

TODO.

## Exercises

TODO — planned: build the VS slice (VSRESU units) in
[exercises/sdtm-findings.qmd](../exercises/sdtm-findings.qmd).

## Comprehension Check

TODO.
